name: Canning Peas (Green or English, Shelled)
recipeInstructions:
1. Select filled pods containing young, tender, sweet seeds. Discard diseased pods.
2. Shell and wash peas.
3. Hot pack: Cover with boiling water. Boil 2 minutes in a saucepan. Fill jars loosely with hot peas and add cooking liquid, leaving 1-inch headspace.
4. Raw pack: Fill jars with raw peas and add boiling water, leaving 1-inch headspace. Do not shake or press down peas.
5. Adjust lids and process according to the method of canning used (see Tables 1 and 2).
Table 1. Recommended process time for Peas in a dial-gauge pressure canner.
Style of Pack: Hot and Raw. Jar Size: Pints or Quarts. Process Time: 40 minutes. Canner Pressure (PSI) at Altitudes of 0 - 2,000 ft: 11 lb.
Table 2. Recommended process time for Peas in a weighted-gauge pressure canner.
Style of Pack: Hot and Raw. Jar Size: Pints or Quarts. Process Time: 40 minutes. Canner Pressure (PSI) at Altitudes of 0 - 1,000 ft: 10 lb. Above 1,000 ft: 15 lb.
